name: itis.gov
title: ITIS.gov | Integrated Taxonomic Information System (ITIS)
description: The Integrated Taxonomic Information System (ITIS, www.itis.gov) partners with specialists from around the world to assemble scientific names and their taxonomic relationships and distributes that data openly through publicly available software. The ITIS mission is to communicate a comprehensive taxonomy of global species that enables biodiversity information to be discovered, indexed, and connected across all human endeavors. ITIS is made up of 11 active MOU partners committed to improving and continually updating scientific and common names of all seven Kingdoms of Life (Archaea, Bacteria, Protozoa, Chromista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia).

name: albedomap.lbl.gov
title: Los Angeles Rooftop Albedo | Berkeley Lab
description: At noon on a clear summer day in the United States, a flat surface receives about 1000 watts of sunlight per square meter. Traditional dark roofs strongly absorb this sunlight, heating both the building and the surrounding air. This increases energy use in air conditioned buildings, and makes non-air conditioned buildings less comfortable. Hot dark roofs also aggravate urban heat islands by warming the air flowing over the roof, and contribute to global warming by radiating heat into the atmosphere.

name: bbs.llnl.gov
title: Biochemical and Biophysical Systems Group
description: The Biochemical and Biophysical Systems Group’s scientists use cutting-edge, multi-scale, in silico simulations to tackle problems in biology. Learn more about the group's members and research.

name: bioams.llnl.gov
title: BioAMS
description: The National User Resource for Biological Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (BioAMS) at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory supports advancements in bioscience by providing researchers with innovative AMS techniques to address biomedical challenges, including therapeutics development and the study of pathogens and toxins. As pioneers in using AMS for biomedical applications, the program has served the scientific community for nearly 30 years, continuously enhancing its capabilities.

name: bioresilience.llnl.gov
title: Center for Predictive Bioresilience
description: Explore the Center for Predictive Bioresilience’s unique approach to research and development in relation to existing and emerging biological threats.

name: biosecurity.llnl.gov
title: Biosecurity, Assessments, and Materials
description: The Biosecurity, Assessments, and Meterials Group delivers intelligence-informed, science-based solutions to problems affecting human health and national security. Learn more about their research and the people in the group.

name: bio-sfa.llnl.gov
title: µBiospheres Scientific Focus Area
description: The µBiospheres Scientific Focus Area program investigates phototroph–heterotroph interactions in bioenergy systems like microalgae and C4 grasses, focusing on their productivity, resource flux balance, and microbial influences. Utilizing advanced techniques such as single-cell analyses and system-scale \'omics, the program aims to develop predictive frameworks for optimizing sustainable bioenergy.

name: bnm.llnl.gov
title: BioNanomaterials Group
description: The BioNanomaterials Group conducts interdisciplinary research in nanoscience and nanotechnology to address national biosecurity challenges. Its work encompasses developing advanced materials like carbon nanotube membranes that guard against chemical and biological threats, vaccine delivery systems using nanolipoprotein technology, and innovative diagnostic and imaging technologies for disease detection and bioanalytical applications.

name: bout.llnl.gov
title: Bout++
description: Bout++ is a modular C++ framework for simulating fluid and plasma dynamics in curvilinear geometries, emphasizing reduced plasma fluid models and enabling testing of numerical methods. It is developed collaboratively by LLNL, the University of York, and other international partners.

name: cams.llnl.gov
title: Center for Accelerator Mass Spectrometry
description: The Center for Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (CAMS) is a specialized facility at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory that utilizes advanced isotope ratio measurement and ion beam analytical techniques to address scientific and national security challenges.

name: carbon-economy.llnl.gov
title: Engineering the Carbon Economy
description: The LLNL Carbon Initiative focuses on advancing Carbon Dioxide Removal (CDR) technologies to support negative CO₂ emissions, mitigate environmental changes, and foster the development of a sustainable carbon economy through innovation, research, and understanding of associated challenges.

name: cgsr.llnl.gov
title: Center for Global Security Research
description: The Center for Global Security Research focuses on advancing strategic deterrence, analyzing major power rivalry, addressing regional security challenges, fostering long-term competitive strategies, and developing cooperative measures to reduce global dangers. It engages experts, publishes research, hosts events, and offers opportunities for fellows and interns in the field of national security.

name: cog.llnl.gov
title: COG: A High Fidelity Multi-Particle Transport Code
description: COG is a high-fidelity, Monte Carlo-based multi-particle transport code developed by Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, capable of simulating particle types like neutrons, photons, electrons, and more for advanced nuclear analysis and radiation transport.

name: combustion.llnl.gov
title: Combustion
description: The page provides an in-depth overview of the development, validation, and application of chemical kinetic models for combustion processes. It explains how mechanisms are created using ab initio calculations, reaction rate measurements, and pathway knowledge to simulate fuel pyrolysis and oxidation. The page also discusses the use of surrogate fuels for practical engine simulations, highlighting advancements in diesel and gasoline surrogate models tailored to match specific fuel properties.

name: computing.llnl.gov
title: | Computing
description: Computing at LLNL advances scientific discovery through foundational and innovative research; mission-driven data science; complex modeling, simulation, and analysis on powerful supercomputers; and creative technologies and software solutions. Everything at Livermore is Team Science. Thus, Computing is at the heart of many of LLNL’s most compelling national security and scientific efforts

name: contenthub.llnl.gov
title: Content Hub
description: The page provides guidance on identifying official and secure U.S. government websites, explaining the significance of .gov domains and HTTPS connections for safely sharing sensitive information.

name: controlbanding.llnl.gov
title: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
description: The website focuses on promoting Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory\'s Control Banding (CB) strategies for assessing and managing workplace exposure risks, particularly in the absence of concrete toxicological data. It highlights tools like the CB Nanotool and multidisciplinary approaches such as the Risk Level Based Management System, providing resources and downloads to support the application and validation of these methods within nanotechnology operations.

name: csl.llnl.gov
title: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
description: The Conflict Simulation Laboratory (CSL) provides advanced modeling and simulation tools, including JCATS and JLOD, to enable high-resolution, entity-level conflict simulations for U.S. military, domestic, and international partners. These tools support force interaction modeling at global and localized levels and are critical for training, analysis, and operational planning.

name: cyber-internship.llnl.gov
title: Cybersecurity Summer Institute
description: The Cybersecurity and Critical Infrastructure Summer Institute offers advanced undergraduate, graduate students, and recent graduates hands-on summer internships at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL). Participants collaborate on projects to enhance the resilience of U.S. infrastructure against cyber, physical, and natural threats, while gaining mentorship, technical expertise, and exposure to interdisciplinary teams working on national security challenges.

name: cybersecurity.llnl.gov
title: LLNL Cybersecurity
description: The Cyber Security Program (CSP) at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, led by Matthew Myrick, implements a comprehensive strategy to safeguard intellectual property, sensitive data, and systems critical to the organization\'s operations. CSP focuses on compliance with DOE and NNSA security policies, cybersecurity training, system certification, inter-agency coordination, threat analysis, incident response, and forensics.

name: data-science.llnl.gov
title: Data Science Institute
description: The homepage of the Data Science Institute highlights innovative data science research, career opportunities, and educational programs. Featured content includes updates on the Data Science Summer Institute, recent breakthroughs in artificial intelligence, and details about upcoming seminars, events, and initiatives like the Open Data Initiative and Precision Medicine advancements.

name: dus.llnl.gov
title: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
description: The Donation, Utilization, and Sales (DUS) group manages the disposition of excess Department of Energy property, facilitating transfers to government agencies, universities, school districts, and nonprofits. It also conducts sealed bid sales of residual property to employees and the public, generating funds to offset operating costs.

name: eap.llnl.gov
title: Employee Assistance Program (EAP) at LLNL
description: LLNL’s Employee Assistance Program offers confidential counseling, consultation, and resources to support employee well‑being at work and at home.

name: ebg.llnl.gov
title: Environmental Biology Group
description: The Environmental Biology Group develops and deploys advanced capabilities to detect, sample, and respond to environmental biological threats. Their expertise spans molecular biology, microbiology, ecology, and chemistry, with projects focused on counterterrorism, biosurveillance, and ecological impact analysis, including mobile laboratory systems and innovative diagnostic methods.

name: ebit.llnl.gov
title: Electron Beam Ion Trap at LLNL
description: The Electron Beam Ion Trap (EBIT) at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL) is a groundbreaking device for creating and trapping highly charged ions using a high current density electron beam. Pioneered at LLNL, EBIT facilitates advanced research in atomic, plasma, and astrophysics by enabling the production of highly charged ions, including bare uranium, under laboratory conditions.

name: eehpcwg.llnl.gov
title: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
description: The Energy Efficient High Performance Computing Working Group (EE HPC WG) promotes sustainable science by advocating energy conservation, efficient HPC design, and best practices while fostering collaboration and community action within the HPC industry. Its mission is to minimize environmental impact and optimize energy efficiency in HPC centers.

name: energetics.llnl.gov
title: Energetic Materials Center
description: The Energetic Materials Center (EMC) at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory specializes in the research and development of non-nuclear high-explosive materials to support national security, conducting advanced studies and predictive modeling on energetic materials such as explosives, propellants, and pyrotechnics. EMC's multidisciplinary teams address challenges in material behavior, chemical assessments, and innovative formulations to counter evolving threats.

name: engineering.llnl.gov
title: Engineering
description: Engineering is critical to programs and departments across Lawrence Livermore National Lab, enabling them to deliver on our shared national security mission.

name: genomics.llnl.gov
title: Genomics Group
description: The Genomics Group works on the leading edge of microbial genomics to develop and apply platforms that detect and characterize pathogens and microbiomes toward maintaining human health, agricultural security, and environmental safety. Learn more about our research and group members.

name: guide.llnl.gov
title: Generative Unconstrained Intelligent Drug Engineering (GUIDE)
description: The Generative Unconstrained Intelligent Drug Engineering (GUIDE) program leverages AI and supercomputing to accelerate drug discovery and enhance biodefense. It aims to develop cost-effective medical countermeasures rapidly, address emerging biothreats, and create a strategic framework to improve preparedness and response capabilities.

name: heds-center.llnl.gov
title: High Energy Density Science Center
description: The High Energy Density Science (HEDS) Center at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL) advances research on matter and energy in extreme conditions through collaboration, educational programs, access to experimental resources, and opportunities for early-career scientists. HEDS supports LLNL's nuclear deterrence mission, delivering insights essential for national security while fostering scientific innovation.

name: high-pressure.llnl.gov
title: Equilibrium Physics at Extreme Conditions
description: The High Pressure Physics Group at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory advances the understanding of materials under extreme conditions of pressure, temperature, and strain rate through experimental and theoretical research. The group's focus includes synthesizing and characterizing novel materials using state-of-the-art technologies in support of programs for the Department of Energy, Department of Defense, and other scientific initiatives.

name: hpc4energyinnovation.llnl.gov
title: HPC4EnergyInnovation (HPC4EI)
description: The HPC4EnergyInnovation (HPC4EI) initiative connects industry with U.S. Department of Energy National Labs to leverage high-performance computing for advancements in energy efficiency, manufacturing processes, and materials innovation, fostering economic growth and technological progress.

name: hpcic.llnl.gov
title: HPC Innovation Center
description: The High Performance Computing Innovation Center (HPCIC) fosters collaboration and innovation in the field of high-performance computing (HPC), leveraging Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory’s expertise and resources. It promotes open-source software, cloud-friendly tutorials, and partnerships to address complex challenges and support cutting-edge scientific research and national security applications.

name: hpc.llnl.gov
title: | HPC @ LLNL
description: At the Department of Energy (DOE) facilities such as Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL), high-performance computing (HPC) has become the “third pillar” of research, joining theory and experiment as an equal partner.

name: ins.llnl.gov
title: INS
description: The INS webpage outlines the Office of International Nuclear Security\'s commitment to mitigating nuclear threats by leveraging expertise to develop technical solutions and foster collaboration across the nuclear security community. It emphasizes the importance of clear, consistent messaging to support its critical mission and uphold its global reputation.

name: ipo.llnl.gov
title: | Innovation and Partnerships Office
description: The Innovation and Partnerships Office serves as a focal point for LLNL engagement with industry.

name: jlf.llnl.gov
title: Jupiter Laser Facility
description: The Jupiter Laser Facility (JLF) is a specialized user facility at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory dedicated to high-energy-density, fusion energy, and laser science research. As part of the LaserNetUS network, JLF partners with academia and the scientific community to advance experimental techniques, develop secondary photon and particle sources, and foster early-career scientist training through hands-on access to its laser platforms, including Janus, Titan, and COMET.

name: kwla.llnl.gov
title: Kenneth G. Wilson Award
description: The annual Kenneth G. Wilson Award for Excellence in Lattice Field Theory recognizes physicists who have made recent, outstanding contributions to lattice field theory. Learn more about the award and view a list of recipients.

name: lasermaterials.llnl.gov
title: Materials for Laser Systems
description: The webpage showcases a team specializing in developing advanced materials, processing, and characterization techniques for optics and targets used in laser systems, including the National Ignition Facility. It highlights their focus areas, such as polymer materials, additive manufacturing, optical finishing, and laser–matter interactions, and invites exploration of their team and projects.

name: lasers.llnl.gov

Their research explores molecular assembly at the nanoscale, material structure-properties relationships, and quantum effects to advance materials science, using state-of-the-art tools to support national security and foundational science research.

name: dataverse.jpl.nasa.gov
bureau: NASA Office of the Chief Information Officer
title: JPL Open Repository
description: The JPL Open Repository (JOR) replaced the JPL Technical Report Server (TRS) in 2023. It is a repository for digital copies of technical publications authored by JPL employees. It includes journal articles (the Final Accepted Version), meeting papers, presentations, and other publications cleared for external distribution from 1992 to the present. It also includes research datasets from 2022 on.  Years covered: 1992 to the present.  1992-2021 records were originally on the JPL Technical Report Server (TRS) on Dspace system; those records were migrated to Dataverse in 2022.  Records with publication date 2022 or later are native to the Dataverse. Data sets: 2022 to present There are a few pre-1992 records. Those were added to the system on a case-by-case request basis.  Persistent IDs (PIDs): 1992-2021 records have Handles 2022 and later records have Digital Object Identifiers (DOIs).

description: The NESC Academy enables effective knowledge capture and transfer, ensuring technical information remains viable and accessible. It provides a forum for the NASA community to gain critical knowledge to aid professional development and support the NASA mission. The NESC Academy presents live and on-demand content from researchers, engineers, and field experts in 21 technical disciplines relevant to the design, development, test, and operation of NASA programs and projects. title: Planetary Protection | Home
description: JPL's Biotechnology and Planetary Protection Group (BPPG) has had a longstanding involvement in mission implementation, and more recently, space microbiology research and technology development. The team ensures that spacecraft meet stringent cleanliness requirements to prevent forward contamination (microbial contamination of the solar system by spacecraft that we launch from Earth) and backward contamination (extraterrestrial contamination of the Earth and Moon by way of sample return missions). In support of this implementation role, the BPP Group seeks to advance spacecraft cleanliness, sterilization, and validation technologies for NASA's solar system exploration missions.

name: brd.nci.nih.gov
agency: Department of Health and Human Services
title: Biospecimen Research Database - Biospecimen Research Database
description: The Biospecimen Research Database (BRD) is a free and publicly accessible database that contains a literature repository of peer-reviewed articles in human Biospecimen Science and a library of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) utilized and contributed by established biobanking organizations. This resource can be used to (i) help identify and define precise relationships between biospecimen handling and the quality and reproducibility of data for cancer research, (ii) facilitate the development of evidence-based SOPs, and (iii) promote harmonization of specimen handling practices across biobanks.  Visitors are encouraged to search, download, comment on, and contribute to the database. The BRD is a product of the National Cancer Institute (NCI)'s Biorepositories and Biospecimen Research Branch (BBRB).

name: actev.nist.gov
bureau: National Institute of Standards and Technology
title: content
description: The Activity Extended Video (ActEV) challenge main focus is on human activity detection in multi-camera video streams. Activity detection has been an active research area in computer vision in recent years. The ability to detect human activities is an important task in computer vision due to its potential in a wide range of applications such as public safety and security, crime  prevention, traffic monitoring and control, eldercare/childcare, human-computer interaction, human-robot interaction, smart homes, hospital activity monitoring, and many more. Here, by activity detection, we mean the detection of visual events (people/objects engaged in particular activities) in a large collection of video data. The ActEV challenge (https://actev.nist.gov) that we are currently running is based on the VIRAT V1

name: ambench2022.nist.gov
bureau: National Institute of Standards and Technology
title: AMBench 2022
description: The NIST Materials Data Curation System (MDCS) provides a means for capturing, sharing, and transforming materials data into a structured format that is XML based amenable to transformation to other formats. The data are organized using user-selected templates encoded in XML Schema. These templates are used to create data entry forms. The documents are saved in a non-relational (NoSQL) database, namely MongoDB. The data can be searched and retrieved by a template-driven web-based form or by a RESTful API call. The system also enables the interconnection of MDCS repositories for federated searches.
